entries.name: FutureBase
entries.code:
FutureBase = ref object of RootObj
  error*: ref Exception      ## Stored exception
  errorStackTrace*: string
  when not defined(release) or defined(futureLogging):
entries.description: Untyped future.

entries.name: Future
entries.description: Typed future.

entries.name: FutureError
entries.code:
FutureError = object of Defect
  cause*: FutureBase

entries.name: NimAsyncContinueSuffix
entries.description: For internal usage. Do not use.

entries.name: getCallSoonProc
entries.description: Get current implementation of callSoon.

entries.name: setCallSoonProc
entries.description: Change current implementation of callSoon. This is normally called when dispatcher from asyncdispatcher is initialized.

entries.name: callSoon
entries.description:
Call cbproc "soon".

If async dispatcher is running, cbproc will be executed during next dispatcher tick.

If async dispatcher is not running, cbproc will be executed immediately.

entries.name: newFuture
entries.description:
Creates a new future.

Specifying fromProc, which is a string specifying the name of the proc that this future belongs to, is a good habit as it helps with debugging.

entries.name: newFutureVar
entries.description:
Create a new FutureVar. This Future type is ideally suited for situations where you want to avoid unnecessary allocations of Futures.

Specifying fromProc, which is a string specifying the name of the proc that this future belongs to, is a good habit as it helps with debugging.

entries.name: clean
entries.description: Resets the finished status of future.

entries.name: complete
entries.code: proc complete[T](future: Future[T]; val: sink T)
entries.description: Completes future with value val.

entries.name: complete
entries.code:
proc complete(future: Future[void]; val = Future[void].default) {.
    raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

entries.name: complete
entries.description: Completes a FutureVar.

entries.name: complete
entries.code: proc complete[T](future: FutureVar[T]; val: sink T)
entries.description:
Completes a FutureVar with value val.

Any previously stored value will be overwritten.

entries.name: fail
entries.code: proc fail[T](future: Future[T]; error: ref Exception)
entries.description: Completes future with error.

entries.name: addCallback
entries.code:
proc addCallback(future: FutureBase; cb: proc () {.closure, gcsafe.}) {.
    raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Adds the callbacks proc to be called when the future completes.

If future has already completed then cb will be called immediately.

entries.name: addCallback
entries.code:
proc addCallback[T](future: Future[T];
                    cb: proc (future: Future[T]) {.closure, gcsafe.})
entries.description:
Adds the callbacks proc to be called when the future completes.

If future has already completed then cb will be called immediately.

entries.name: callback=
entries.code:
proc callback=(future: FutureBase; cb: proc () {.closure, gcsafe.}) {.
    raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Clears the list of callbacks and sets the callback proc to be called when the future completes.

If future has already completed then cb will be called immediately.

It's recommended to use addCallback or then instead.

entries.name: callback=
entries.code:
proc callback=[T](future: Future[T];
                  cb: proc (future: Future[T]) {.closure, gcsafe.})
entries.description:
Sets the callback proc to be called when the future completes.

If future has already completed then cb will be called immediately.

entries.code:
proc `$`(stackTraceEntries: seq[StackTraceEntry]): string {.
    raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: read
entries.code: proc read[T](future: Future[T] | FutureVar[T]): lent T
entries.description:
Retrieves the value of future. Future must be finished otherwise this function will fail with a ValueError exception.

If the result of the future is an error then that error will be raised.

entries.name: readError
entries.description:
Retrieves the exception stored in future.

An ValueError exception will be thrown if no exception exists in the specified Future.

entries.name: mget
entries.description:
Returns a mutable value stored in future.

Unlike read, this function will not raise an exception if the Future has not been finished.

entries.name: finished
entries.description:
Determines whether future has completed.

True may indicate an error or a value. Use failed to distinguish.

entries.name: failed
entries.code: proc failed(future: FutureBase): bool {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Determines whether future completed with an error.

entries.name: asyncCheck
entries.description:
Sets a callback on future which raises an exception if the future finished with an error.

This should be used instead of discard to discard void futures, or use waitFor if you need to wait for the future's completion.

entries.name: `and`
entries.code: proc `and`[T, Y](fut1: Future[T]; fut2: Future[Y]): Future[void]
entries.description: Returns a future which will complete once both fut1 and fut2 complete.

entries.name: `or`
entries.code: proc `or`[T, Y](fut1: Future[T]; fut2: Future[Y]): Future[void]
entries.description: Returns a future which will complete once either fut1 or fut2 complete.

entries.name: all
entries.description:
Returns a future which will complete once all futures in futs complete. If the argument is empty, the returned future completes immediately.

If the awaited futures are not Future[void], the returned future will hold the values of all awaited futures in a sequence.

If the awaited futures are Future[void], this proc returns Future[void].
